News.title: Cop repeatedly stabbed in NECK while trying to save colleagues in ambush by knife-wielding yob gets award
News.content:
THIS is the grim moment a brave cop is repeatedly stabbed in the neck while trying to save colleagues.

Sergeant Tim Ansell was left with horrifying injuries after hurling himself between the attacker and three officers in Whalley Range, Manchester.





In shocking bodycam footage selfless hero Tim is seen clutching his throat and begging for help after a knife-wielding yob launched a savage ambush.

The terrifying clip from July 2023 shows the aftermath of the attack as the wounded officer struggles to stay on his feet.

Tim can be heard saying: “Put something on my neck here, I’ve been f***ing stabbed here.”

Moments earlier, what began as a routine traffic stop spiralled into chaos.

This was when hooded thug Jacob Brown, 27, suddenly lunged at officers from behind with a knife.

He first targeted Sgt Ansell’s colleague, Sergeant Foster, before the brave cop stepped in.

In a sickening burst of violence, Tim was repeatedly stabbed, suffering open wounds to his neck along with injuries to his arm and body.



Despite his injuries, the officer fought back.

He grappled with the knifeman and was able to grab hold of his upper body to stop him attacking others.

The chaos only ended when PC Marcus Wolstencroft fired a Taser, allowing officers to restrain and handcuff Brown.

Miraculously, no members of the public were hurt.

And in a powerful recognition of his bravery, Sgt Ansell has now been honoured with the King’s Commendation for Bravery.



He received the prestigious award at St James’s Palace this week.

Tim had already picked up a string of accolades alongside his colleagues.

These include the Chief Constable’s Annual Bravery Awards, the John Egerton Bravery Award and the North West Bravery Award.

Police said: “The actions of all three officers ultimately saved each other and meant no members of the public suffered any injuries.”

Brown had no connection to the original crash.



He can be seen on bodycam footage approaching the officers despite not being involved in the crash incident.

Dressed all in black with a hood tightly fastened, he is seen walking down the street near his home before jumping into the road to attack the officers from behind.

PS Foster was struck to the head several times as Brown aimed a knife at his neck.

Brown was armed with a blue-handled craft knife and later admitted attempted murder and three counts of wounding with intent.

He was sentenced in April 2024 to an indefinite hospital order.



Prior to sentencing and while in custody, Brown stabbed a staff member during an induction at HMP Liverpool.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The following month after that incident Brown attacked a prison guard and stabbed him in the neck with a sharp object.

But it is Sgt Ansell’s courage in the face of a brutal, unprovoked attack that has now been rightly recognised after he risked everything to protect his fellow officers.

News.title: Boy, 16, pleads not guilty to murder of schoolgirl, 9, stabbed in chest at home
News.content:
IN DOCK Boy, 16, pleads not guilty to murder of schoolgirl, 9, stabbed in chest at home

A 16-YEAR-old boy has pleaded not guilty to murdering a “fun-loving” nine-year-old girl who was found stabbed to death inside her home.

Little Aria died from a single knife wound in a tragedy that rocked the seaside town of Weston-super-Mare, Somerset, last December.





Cops were scrambled to the family home at around 6.09pm on Monday December 15, arresting a teen on suspicion of murder seven minutes later.

A 16-year-old boy, then 15, who cannot be named for legal reasons, was remanded into youth detention accommodation charged with murder.

Appearing at Bristol Crown Court today, he pleaded not guilty to murder.

Aria had tragically celebrated her ninth birthday with a KPop Demon Hunters party just days before her death.

Her devasted relatives hailed her as “brave” and “kind-hearted”, adding “our hearts are broken in a way we never imagined possible”.

Her mum Victoria Hull, a former soldier and hairstylist, was said to be devastated.

Family on her mum’s side said: “For those who didn’t have the privilege of knowing Aria, she was the most beautiful little soul…

“She loved to sing and dance, and she took such pride in dressing up, always wanting to look just like her mummy.



“The loss of Aria has devastated us beyond words. Our hearts are broken in a way we never imagined possible.”

Hundreds of devastated locals attended a candle-lit vigil on the beach in the days after her tragic death.

Her dad, Tom Thorpe, called his daughter a “special little angel” in an emotional tribute.

He said: “It’s hard to put into words how devastating it is, our little Aria, that you’re up there with the angels looking over us.

“You were such a brave, kind-hearted and a beautiful innocent soul.



“You always put a smile on people’s faces with your wacky ways and just outright madness.

“You always found it so easy to show your affection to others and ensure others around you were happy and never bored!

“A life gone far too early but I hope a life lived well. We all love you dearly. Goodnight darling.”

News.title: Moment hero firefighters save three cats with CPR after rescue from blaze – as fans thank them for ‘never giving up’
News.content:
THIS is the heart racing moment brave firefighters saved four beloved cats from a blazing apartment fire before giving one of the cherished pets CPR.

The dramatic rescue was caught on camera after the animals were discovered trapped inside the severely damaged apartment.





Three of them were unconscious and not breathing when they were found in Jurupa Valley, California.

The firefighters were seen rushing to the animals’ aid, where they immediately started trying to get them to breathe.

Additional firefighting crews and a Riverside County Sheriff’s deputy were filmed joining the rescue effort.

The video showed the cats being brought to the front of the property and laid out on the grass.

The scenes became emotional as the firefighters worked quickly to save the animals, stroking their heads and pumping their chests to get oxygen flowing.

Three oxygen tanks were pulled out in the desperate bid to save the cats.

The video showed rescue workers using oxygen bags to distribute the fresh air into the cats’ lungs.



At one stage, one of the firefighters even began giving one of the cats mouth-to-mouth CPR in the frantic efforts to revive the cats.

After several heart stopping seconds, the furry felines began to show signs of life.

Beginning to regain consciousness with the firefighters feeding them drops of water.

All four cats thankfully survived the horrific ordeal.

The video has sparked endless praise for the rescuers from keen animal lovers, with one person saying it was “the most heroic thing they’ve ever witnessed”.



“Thank you for not giving up. Thank you for not saying ‘it’s a cat…who cares’. Thank you for saving any and every life you can,” one person commented.

Another person added: “You didn’t just save a cat… you saved someone’s whole world.”

A third person said: “Real men care about animals and don’t hunt! And also real men care about all living things. So impressed and touched by this.”

Another person thanked the crews for their efforts.



“Thank you for saving the beautiful kitties. They were already older. One was 18 years old. You guys are a blessing,” they wrote.

It comes after a pack of seven missing pet dogs were seen marching along a busy highway after they were reportedly stolen for illegal meat trade.

The shaggy pack travelled more than 10 miles together before eventually reuniting with their desperate owners.

The animals were spotted moving as a tight group along a road in Changchun, the capital of north-east China’s Jilin province, in footage that quickly went viral online.



The video, first posted online on March 15, shows the dogs – including a golden retriever, Labrador, German shepherd and Pekinese – walking along the highway as overnight temperatures dropped below 0C.

Leading the group was a corgi later identified in Chinese media as Dapang, meaning “big fatty”.

The dogs were eventually traced back to three households in a nearby village.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

News.title: What is the Benidorm weather forecast for the Easter holiday?
News.content:
BENIDORM is a famous coastal resort town on Spain’s Costa Blanca, perfect for a quick getaway holiday this Easter.

Renowned for its vibrant nightlife, the Mediterranean tourist hub is known by many as the “Spanish Las Vegas”.





This year the Easter break falls early, with most schools holding a two-week break from Monday, March 30, 2026, to Friday, April 10.

So if you’re looking to take the kids away on a quick getaway, Benidorm might be the spot for you, but what’s the weather expected to be like?

What is the Benidorm weather for Easter holiday?

With as many as five beaches to enjoy, a stunning Old Town to wander through and cheap prices on arrival.

Benidorm is expected to be popular amongst British and Irish tourists across the Easter break.

According to holiday-weather.com, the weather is expected to be mixed across the next two weeks in the Mediterranean holiday resort.

The temperatures are set to reach highs of around 12°C to 17°C across the Easter holiday, warming up towards the first week of April.



Good Friday (April 3), Holy Saturday (April 4), and Easter Sunday (April 5) are all forecast to reach 15°C, whilst Easter Monday (April 6) is set for highs of 16°C.

The good news is, there is no rain expected until Friday, April 10, however once showers start they are expected to continue, with rain forecast for the next six days afterwards.

News.title: Top fashionista locked in row with couple who bought her £5m London pad secretly blighted by floods & infested with MICE
News.content:
FLOOD FURY Top fashionista locked in row with couple who bought her £5m London pad secretly blighted by floods & infested with MICE

A TOP fashion director is locked in a row with a couple who bought her £5million house that was infested by mice and blighted by floods.

Former Harvey Nichols fashion director Paula Reed denies she used “deceit” to sell a top barrister and his wife her £5.5m Notting Hill house.





She claims knew nothing about alleged puddles in the basement or a mouse infestation at her six-storey architect-designed home.

The house – near the famous Portobello Market – was bought by top commercial barrister Tom Grayson Ford and his wife Jessica in December 2024.

The former Grazia fashion editor, 64, sold her glam pad as she stepped back from her city career and retreated to Cornwall to renovate an old vicarage.

She documented the process in a column for Good Housekeeping magazine.



Ms Reed – who has appeared on TV shows Project Catwalk and 10 Years Younger – is now locked in a court fight with lawyer Mr Grayson Ford and his wife.

They are suing for the right to hand the house back or claim over £1m in damages.

The couple claim Ms Reed deliberately or “recklessly” failed to reveal issues with the property.

It had been revamped decades earlier by her ex-husband, top architect Alfred Munkenbeck.



The couple say that soon after they moved, in their basement flooded, they were plagued by “mice, alive and dead” plus “numerous droppings,” as well as having to deal with a leaky kitchen roof.

They allege that they were promised during the sale process that the multimillion-pound house was “well-renovated and in ‘move in’ condition”.

The multimillionaire couple say that the problems they say they have encountered must have already existed and that the fashionista must have known about them.



But Ms Reed says they could have caused the kitchen leak themselves by ripping out a bespoke green roof of “ferns and ivy,” which had covered that part of the house.

The seven-bedroom three-bathroom six-floor townhouse at the centre of the fight is set on Westbourne Grove in the heart of fashionable Notting Hill.

Numerous famous faces have called the area home over the years, including Damon Albarn, Stella McCartney, Robbie Williams, Sir Elton John, David Cameron, Lindsay Lohan and Harry Styles.

Ms Reed sold the property in December 2024, having already begun a new life in the country after moving to a £1.2m listed vicarage in Morwenstow, Cornwall.

In her defence to the action, lodged with London’s High Court, Ms Reed’s barrister said “there was no flooding at the property, and it did not suffer from any infestation of vermin” when she lived there.



After 2020, the house was rented out and she had received no complains or reports of flooding or vermin from the tenants, he says.

He pointed out that the basement room which the couple says is the epicentre of the “flooding” is a “pump room” for a former swimming pool in the garden, now converted to a pond.

He said it was “not intended for habitation”.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The judge directed that an expert building surveyor should be recruited to investigate the house and give evidence as to “what state the property was in on the date of completion and was there evidence of flooding”.

Unless it settles out of court, the case will return for trial at a later date.

News.title: Watch idiot undertake lorry on layby – but viewers think there’s another driver who needs to be prosecuted
News.content:
THIS is the shocking moment a reckless driver undertakes a lorry using a layby – but viewers think they’ve spotted another driver who’s also in the wrong.

The dashcam footage shows one motorist racing down the slow lane before darting into the layby and popping back out again.





Cops tracked the motorist down and gave them a fine alongside three points on their licence for the dangerous manoeuvre on the A30 in Cornwall.

Devon and Cornwall Police posted the video to social media but it was the motorist who filmed the undertake that got the most attention.

Many users were quick to accuse the driver with the dashcam footage of staying too long in the overtaking lane.

Some comments even called for the motorist to be prosecuted.



One said: “Did the dashcam submitter get prosecuted for lane hogging too as per rule 264 of the highway code?”

Another added: “The camera car should definitely be prosecuted for lane hogging there’s no reason to be in that lane.”

Following the backlash the police road safety team uploaded a longer video which showed the driver had pulled into the lane ahead of overtaking the same lorry.

A spokesperson for the force said people were ignoring the dangerous undertake and instead blaming the more responsible driver.



They asked online users to get some perspective on who the bad driver is in the video.

The statement continued: “Totally ignoring the horrific standard of driving from the car in front.

“In this clip, you can clearly see the camera car was in lane one prior to the incident and moves across to lane two when the slower-moving HGV comes into view.

“Hopefully this will reassure the commenters who somehow felt the camera car was in the wrong.”

The video was submitted anonymously as part of Operation Snap.



The driver who undertook the lorry was identified and later admitted to careless driving.

They received a fine and three penalty points.

Devon and Cornwall Police said the undertake was unacceptable and could have led to a collision.

They urged drivers to be patient, especially on dual carriageways, and wait a few seconds for an opportunity to overtake safely.

News.title: Bombshell twist in haunting Gilgo Beach ‘serial killer’ case as suspect Rex Heuermann ‘preparing to plead GUILTY’
News.content:
ALLEGED Gilgo Beach serial killer Rex Heuermann intends to plead guilty at his next court appearance, sources have said.

The bombshell update suggests the 62-year-old charged with murdering seven women over 17 years is set to change his plea from not guilty on April 8.





Families of the victims and Heuermann’s own family have already been notified of the decision, it has been reported.

The two sources, who have both been involved in the case, spoke on condition of anonymity because the plea has yet to be entered in court.

Lawyers for Heuermann and the victims’ families did not immediately respond to requests for comment.



Heuermann can still change his mind.

The architect and married father of two from Massapequa in Nassau County in Long Island was arrested on July 13, 2023.

He faces seven counts of murder for the grisly killings and dismemberments of Sandra Costilla, Valerie Mack, Jessica Taylor, and Maureen Brainard-Barnes, Melissa Barthelemy, Megan Waterman, and Amber Lynn Costello – the latter four collectively known as the Gilgo Four.

Remains of the victims were found decomposed and in sacks on an isolated stretch of shoreline parkway, not far from Heuermann’s home.



Prosecutors said DNA evidence, cellphone data and evidence found in a search of Heuermann’s Massapequa home connect him to the victims, who were all young women involved in sex work.

Heuermann allegedly killed and dismembered his victims when his second ex-wife, Asa Ellerup, and his two children were out of town on family trips, prosecutors claim.

The trial was set to begin in September.

But Heuermann’s attorneys tried to split the sprawling case into five separate trials due to the differences in the killings.

The defense also contended that jurors would struggle with the “volume and complexity” of the evidence in the case if all seven killings were tried together.



The judge in the case denied the defense’s motion, siding with prosecutors who argued that evidence in the cases overlaps.

News of a suspected Gilgo Beach serial killer first exploded into public view in 2010, when police searching for a missing woman discovered numerous sets of human remains in the scrub along Ocean Parkway, not far from Gilgo Beach.

Over the years, investigators used DNA analysis and other clues to identify the victims.

It took years for investigators to identify Heuermann as a potential suspect.

A renewed investigation into the cold case first identified him as the potential culprit in 2022.



Detectives linked Heuermann to a pickup truck that a witness reported seeing when one of the victims disappeared in 2010.

Then, investigators surveilling Heuermann recovered a pizza crust he’d discarded in the trash and used it to link him to DNA from a hair recovered from one of the victims’ bodies.

Cellphone data additionally showed Heuermann was in contact with some of the victims just before their disappearances.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

A review of his internet searches revealed a sick history of violent torture pornography and information about the investigation into the killings.

Last year, investigators recovered files from Heuermann’s computer that they described as a “blueprint for the killings”, including a series of checklists with “reminders to limit noise, clean the bodies and destroy evidence”.

News.title: When do clocks go forward 2026 and why do we have daylight saving time?
News.content:
DAYS are about to get longer in the UK as the clocks are set to go forward by an hour before we head into summer.

That is at the expense of an hour’s sleep, as this year the clock change will take place at 1am GMT on March 29.





Why do clocks go forward?

The clocks go forward as we make the transition from GMT into British Summer Time (BST), which is also known as Daylight Saving Time (DST).

We go forward an hour so that evenings will become brighter and mornings will be darker as both the sunset and sunrise are pushed back.

Since the spring equinox on March 20, most of the northern hemisphere has seen more than 12 hours of daylight each day.

And days are only expected to gradually extend even further as we head toward summer.

This is set to peak on the longest day of the year which will fall on June 21 – where there will be close to 19 hours of daylight in the far north of the UK.

It’s often argued that a huge positive that comes with BST is that lighter summer evenings improve road safety, reducing traffic accidents at busy commuting times.

However, many argue it’s not worth losing the hour’s sleep as the mild, nationwide jet lag has been known to cause short-term effects on mood, concentration, and mental wellbeing.

The UK isn’t alone in putting the clocks forward, with many countries around the world, especially in Europe and North America, also engaging in daylight saving time.



However, many countries closer to the equator, don’t observe it.

What is daylight saving time?

Daylight Saving Time (DST) is the practice of putting clocks one hour ahead during the summer months.

The concept was first proposed seriously by William Willett in the early 1900s before being first used by Germany in 1916 during World War One, with the aim being to better use daylight hours and conserve energy.

However, the idea was taken one step further by the UK in World War Two as they introduced British Double Summer Time.



This was where clocks were moved two hours ahead of GMT during the summer and remained one hour ahead of GMT in the winter when the clocks were put back again.

But daylight saving is no longer just a wartime measure, becoming a lasting feature here in the UK and in many other countries and continuing to shape how people experience daylight throughout the year.

News.title: 'I found something in shower then had 35pc chance of death'
News.content:
'I found something in shower then had 35pc chance of death'

Vicky Keep, 51, was wondering how she would tell her children

View 4 Images Vicky having chemotherapy for the first time (Image: PA Real Life)

A mum-of-two was left wondering how she'd tell her children when she realised she had a '35 per cent chance of dying'. But now she is commemorating the 10th anniversary of her cancer diagnosis by embarking on a canoeing expedition along Sweden's Harken river, wild camping throughout the journey, as cancer has taught her to "not take life for granted anymore".

‌



Vicky Keep, 51, was diagnosed with stage three lobular breast cancer in 2016, after discovering "quite a big lump" in her breast while in the shower. After undergoing a single mastectomy, six months of chemotherapy and daily radiotherapy, she was informed she had overcome cancer in January 2018.

‌



Alongside two close friends, Vicky will paddle the entire length of the Harken river in northern Sweden this September, tackling rapids, portaging through dams, and surviving in the wilderness for eight days, in a challenge she describes as "not going to be a breeze, but something that will feel like a big achievement when we do complete it".

‌



She aims to raise £10,000 for Odyssey, an "incredible charity" that supports people with cancer to rediscover their zest for life. "Reaching 10 years is something that I've got to celebrate," Vicky said.

Vicky, who lives in Whitstable, Kent, and works in catering, found a lump in her left breast in May 2016 when, after a friend mentioned she had discovered a lump in her own breast, she realised she'd never examined herself before. That evening, while showering, she felt "quite a big lump", but presumed it "can't be anything sinister", as she believed breast cancer lumps were typically pea-sized.

View 4 Images Vicky underwent a gruelling 19 months of cancer treatment and surgeries (Image: PA Real Life)

‌



She waited a month, thinking it "might just go", but eventually visited her GP. She was quickly referred for a mammogram, recalling "that was the first point when I thought it might be something sinister, because there were two nurses in there who were looking at each other with a mild look of concern".

After an ultrasound and biopsies, she attended a consultant appointment at 4pm on June 22, 2016, assuming that "they're not going to tell me any bad news that late in the day". Instead, she was diagnosed with lobular breast cancer that had spread to her lymph nodes.

"You just don't see it coming," said Vicky, a mum of two daughters aged 17 and 21 who is celebrating her 25th wedding anniversary this year with her husband, Gary.

‌



"I remember just sitting there hearing these words: 'Sorry, it is breast cancer'. They'd also found a tumour in my armpit as well, and it was quite a large tumour.

"My first question to him was: 'How am I going to tell my girls?'. They were seven and just turned 12 at the time... you immediately just assume you're going to die. And I did have one of the most aggressive types of cancer. It was stage three, grade three. So when you actually looked at the statistics, the five-year survival rates were actually quite low."

Vicky underwent a mastectomy three weeks later, during which her affected lymph nodes were also removed, and six weeks following the operation she started chemotherapy. Following six months of chemotherapy, she took a month's break before commencing daily radiotherapy for a month.

‌



View 4 Images Vicky wanted to ‘do something big’ to mark a decade from her diagnosis (Image: PA Real Life)

She was informed that with the surgery, chemotherapy and radiotherapy, her five-year survival rate was approximately 65 per cent, but "the way I was looking at that was like, well, that's a 35 per cent chance of me dying". However, in January 2018, Vicky received the news that there was No Evidence of Disease (Ned) following a punishing 19 months of cancer treatment and operations.

"Now life is completely different. I very rarely think about cancer, but I didn't think I'd be here to be saying that," she said.

‌



Throughout the past eight years, Vicky has undertaken numerous challenges to raise funds for cancer charities, such as Cancer Research's Walk All Over Cancer, where she and her family walked 10,000 steps daily throughout March 2018. To commemorate the 10th anniversary of her diagnosis, Vicky wanted to "do something big", something that would generate a game-changing sum of money for a lesser-known cancer charity.

Vicky first encountered Odyssey shortly after her cancer treatment, when she was invited to one of its retreats for individuals living with or who have survived cancer. The charity organises residential weeks away for people battling cancer, those who have completed treatment and are rebuilding their lives, or those facing a terminal diagnosis, where they "challenge themselves to do things which are out of their comfort zone".

"Had I known what was on the agenda for that week, I would never have signed up for it..." Vicky confessed, choosing not to reveal the itinerary to preserve the surprise for future attendees. "But it was such an incredible week that when I came back, it just kind of felt like my life was going to be okay. I might not survive five, 10 years, but I've got to make sure that the life that I'm living is worthwhile and counted. It gave me a completely different outlook on life."

‌



When Vicky discovered that Odyssey was organising a canoe expedition down Sweden's Harken river in September 2026, she decided it was precisely the challenge she needed and even enlisted two close friends to join her.

"I phoned my best friend... She was like, '100% – 10 years is something to be celebrated. Let's do something that will challenge us both'," Vicky shared.

Another close mate, who Vicky got to know through her local soul choir and discovered was battling cancer at the same time, undergoing the same surgery and treatments, also agreed to participate in the challenge. Besides canoeing the river's 150-kilometre stretch, the team will encounter various obstacles along the way.

‌



View 4 Images Vicky will canoe the Harken river with two of her close friends (Image: PA Real Life)

"There will be some small rapids in areas, and some dams that we have to use porterage to move the canoes between," Vicky detailed, adding that she was informed by someone who has previously canoed the river that "at one point you have to carry all your equipment three-and-a-half kilometres to the other side, where you have to then put your boats back in."

They'll be wild camping and cooking on open fires during their journey, and while temperatures hover around 12 degrees during the day in September, they can plummet close to zero at night. "I've never done wild camping," Vicky confessed.

‌



"It's not just wild camping. There are no toilets, so it's wild pooing and all that! That is panicking me beyond what I can imagine, I'm like, Oh, God, this is way out of my comfort zone!"

Vicky and her pals will kick off their training in April, and have been advised to do plenty of front crawl swimming to build up the muscles they need for rowing. They'll also do some sea swimming, "because that's more challenging and it's cold", as well as taking the kayaks out for extended sessions.

The team's goal is to raise a minimum of £1,500 each, which would cover their participation costs, but they aspire to raise a total of £10,000 so they can contribute some much-needed funds to Odyssey.

Article continues below

Vicky's fundraising page is available on GoFundMe.

News.title: National Lottery down LIVE: Outage on app sees thousands unable to view tickets
News.content:
The National Lottery mobile app is down for several customers this morning, with many being unable to log in and view their tickets.

Over 1,000 people have reported issues with the platform since around 6am this morning, according to Downdetector.

"We are currently aware that players are unable to sign into their accounts. We are looking into this urgently," a spokesperson for Allwyn, the operator of the National Lottery, said. "We are sorry for the inconvenience and thank players for their patience."

News.title: Policeman repeatedly stabbed protecting colleague during routine traffic stop
News.content:
Policeman repeatedly stabbed protecting colleague during routine traffic stop

Greater Manchester Police Sergeant Tim Ansell was repeatedly stabbed after bravely placing himself between a knifeman and his colleague - who was also attacked during the routine traffic stop

Bodycam footage captures the moment Sgt Tim Ansell in the neck

The horrifying moment a police officer was repeatedly stabbed after tackling a knifeman who lunged at his colleague during a routine traffic stop was caught on camera.

‌



Greater Manchester Police Sergeant Tim Ansell was knifed when he stepped in to protect a fellow officer during the armed attack in Whalley Range on July 26, 2023. A hooded man, identified as Jacob Brown, 27, pounced onto officers from behind with a knife, attacking Ansell's colleague, Sergeant Foster, first.

‌



Bodycam footage showed the officer staggering to his feet and calling for help. "Put something on my neck here, I've been f***ing stabbed here," he was heard telling a colleague.

‌



View 2 Images The incident was caught on police bodycam footage (Image: MEN Media)

Sgt Ansell then bravely placed himself between the knifeman and Sgt Frost, preventing the officer from further harm. Sgt Ansell was stabbed repeatedly, sustaining open wounds to his neck and injuries to his arm and body, reports Manchester Evening News.

‌



He eventually took hold of the knifeman's upper body, preventing another potential attack. Colleague PC Marcus Wolstencroft deployed a Taser to restrain and handcuff Brown, who was then arrested and taken into custody.

All three police officers were later awarded for their bravery with the Chief Constable's Annual Bravery Awards, the John Egerton Bravery Award from GMP's Police Federation and the North West Bravery Award.

View 2 Images Sergeant Timothy Ansell is decorated with the King's Commendation for Bravery (Image: PA)

Article continues below

Sgt Ansell was then awarded the King's Commendation for Bravery. Greater Manchester Police said he received the award at St. James' Palace in London on Wednesday, March 25.

"The actions of all three officers ultimately saved each other and meant no members of the public suffered any injuries," the force said.

Brown, who had been carrying a blue-handled craft knife and had no connection to the original collision, was sentenced in April 2024 to an indefinite hospital order after pleading guilty to attempted murder with three counts of wounding with intent.

News.title: Kennedy family tragedies from curse of assassinations to harrowing plane death
News.content:
Kennedy family tragedies from curse of assassinations to harrowing plane death

The Kennedy family have faced decades of tragedies dubbed the 'Kennedy Curse' - from the JFK assassination to plane crashes and personal struggles that have devastated America's most famous political dynasty

View 10 Images The Kennedy family have faced heartache over the years (Image: Facebook/Getty)

The Kennedy family were faced with another devastating loss in December after Tatiana Schlossberg, the granddaughter of former US President John F Kennedy, died aged 35 after being diagnosed with cancer.

‌



Schlossberg was the daughter of designer Edwin Schlossberg and diplomat Caroline Kennedy, and openly spoke about being given a year to live after receiving an acute myeloid leukaemia diagnosis in May 2024 after giving birth to her second child.

‌



The Kennedy family have faced several family losses over the years, with the final episode of Love Story - a series depicting the romance and sudden deaths of John F Kennedy Jr and Carolyn Bessette - set to air tonight. In 2019, Robert F Kennedy's granddaughter Saoirse Kennedy Hill died of a suspected drug overdose. Her family revealed their "hearts were shattered" by the 22-year-old's death, adding that her life was "filled with hope, promise and love."

‌



JetBlue flight makes emergency U-turn as captain gives very unusual announcement READ MORE :

View 10 Images Tatiana revealed her cancer diagnosis shortly before her untimely death (Image: Ben Stansall/AFP via Getty Images)

Two of the family's most harrowing ordeals - the assassinations of President John F Kennedy and his younger brother Robert - were extraordinarily public affairs, with news of both events reverberating across the globe.

President John F Kennedy was shot dead while waving to crowds from an open-top car travelling through Dallas, Texas, alongside his wife Jackie.

‌



Just two hours after the assassination, Jackie was compelled to witness his successor's inauguration while still wearing the pink suit bearing his bloodstains.

Prior to that historic moment, which was captured on camera, she wiped some of the blood from her face, a decision she immediately came to regret.

She later said: "One second later, I thought, 'Why did I wash the blood off?' I should have left it there; let them see what they've done."

‌



His killer, Lee Harvey Oswald, was shot dead at a police station just two days later. Merely five years on, JFK's younger brother and attorney general Robert was also assassinated following his victory in the California Democratic primary.

His killer, Sirhan Sirhan, remains incarcerated in a Californian prison. Here, we take a look back at some of the victims of the so-called 'Kennedy Curse'.

JFK son's plane death

View 10 Images John F Kennedy Jr, with his wife, Carolyn Bessette, at the White House the year before their deaths (Image: Getty)

‌



John F Kennedy Jr, together with his wife, Carolyn Bessette, and sister-in-law, Lauren Bessette, perished in a plane crash on July 16, 1999.

The three were en route to Martha's Vineyard, Massachusetts, when the aircraft JFK Jr was piloting plunged into the Atlantic Ocean.

An inquiry into the disaster concluded that Kennedy had become disoriented while flying over the featureless water at night and lost control of the plane.

‌



Just six weeks before the flight, Kennedy had fractured his foot and had only had his cast removed the day before the fatal journey. He was still walking with a limp.

Furthermore, the trio's departure for their trip to Martha's Vineyard had been delayed.

They had been due to set off during daylight hours, but Lauren was held up at work and subsequent traffic congestion en route to the airfield meant they didn't take off until after dark.

‌



Possibly in a bid to makeup time, Kennedy opted for the shortest route to his destination across a 30-mile stretch of open water, rather than following the coastline, which would have offered ground-level lighting.

Following the deaths of Kennedy, his wife and sister-in-law, his uncle, Ted, said: "WE dared to think, in that other Irish phrase, that this John Kennedy would live to comb grey hair, with his beloved Carolyn by his side.

"But like his father he had every gift but years." Kennedy was just 38 when he died.

‌



Ted Kennedy's near miss

View 10 Images Ted Kennedy was almost killed in a plane crash that claimed the lives of two others (Image: Time & Life Pictures/Getty Image)

In 1964, 35 years before his nephew's fatal plane crash, Ted Kennedy was caught up in a tragedy in the skies.

JFK's brother, and fellow politician, was travelling to accept the nomination for a full-term in the Senate when the so-called 'curse' struck.

‌



Ted, then 32, was on board with Democratic Convention keynote speaker Senator Birch Bayh, Bayh's wife Marvella, his legislative aide Edward Moss, and pilot Edwin Zimny.

Just three miles from the runway, and only minutes after take-off, the aircraft clipped some trees and plummeted into an orchard.

The plane had been flying too low, and both Moss and Zimny were killed outright. Senator Bayh escaped with barely a scratch and, after pulling his wife clear of the wreckage, went back to rescue Ted.

‌



Ted had suffered three broken vertebrae, two broken ribs and a collapsed lung - yet he pulled through. He spent the following five months recovering in hospital.

Mary Kennedy's tragic suicide

View 10 Images Robert and Mary Kennedy, Jr. at The Food Allergy Ball at The Plaza Hotel in New York City (Image: Getty)

The troubled former wife of Robert F Kennedy Jr was discovered hanged in a barn at her home in 2012 by her devastated housekeeper.

‌



Architect and designer Mary, 52, had been living as a recluse since her marriage collapsed two years earlier. In a statement, the family said she "inspired us with her kindness, her love, her gentle soul and generous spirit".

They added: "Mary was a genius at ­friendship, a tremendously gifted architect and a pioneer and relentless advocate of green design who enhanced her cutting-edge, energy efficient creations with ­exquisite taste and style.

"Our heart goes out to her children who she loved without ­reservation."

‌



As her devastated family came to terms with the shocking news, a portrait emerged of a woman who had struggled with drugs and alcohol in the years since she joined the Kennedy family in 1994.

She had known the clan for years through her close bond with Robert Jr's sister Kerry, whom she had met at boarding school, and Mary served as her maid of honour in 1990.

In a statement following the tragic news, Kerry said: "She's my best friend. Our whole family is devastated by the loss and we ­appreciate everyone's prayers."

‌



Mary had endured some troubled times, including her arrest in 2010 on a drink-driving charge. She was also charged later that year with driving under the influence of drugs, though the case was subsequently dismissed.

Three years prior to that, Mary hit the headlines once more when her husband rushed her to hospital for treatment, but she resisted and fled screaming from the vehicle.

A witness recalled: "I remember she was acting kind of out of it, kind of crazy, she was running into the street and flailing her arms around. He was trying to restrain her." Mary wed Bobby in a ceremony held aboard a vessel on the Hudson River. He filed for divorce in 2010.

‌



Her former husband, a well-known environmentalist and the third-eldest of Bobby's 11 children, had been residing in California and dating TV actress Cheryl Hines.

Mary was laid to rest near her husband's aunt and uncle Eunice and Sargent Shriver, but seven weeks later her body was exhumed and reburied in a Cape Cod cemetery 700 feet from her original grave.

Robert Kennedy Jr reportedly requested that Mary Kennedy be reburied on a hilltop surrounded by vacant plots he is in negotiations to purchase.

‌



Rosemary Kennedy

View 10 Images Rosemary Kennedy had brain surgery that went wrong (Image: Getty)

Prone to mood swings and violent outbursts as a teenager, her parents were advised she would benefit from brain surgery.

However, the lobotomy performed on the 23 year old left her with the mental capacity of an infant. Incapacitated for the remainder of her life, she passed away in a care home in 2005, aged 86.

‌



War hero death

JFK's elder brother, Joe Kennedy Jr, perished in an air disaster during World War Two. He had volunteered to pilot a perilous and classified bombing mission in France in 1944.

However, during the dangerous mission, his bomber aircraft was struck by two in-flight explosions, killing both pilots, including Joe.

Killed in a storm

View 10 Images Kathleen Kennedy was killed in a plane crash four years after her brother (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Just four years after losing his brother in an air tragedy, JFK lost his sister Kathleen, who was fondly known as Kick.

She had married William Cavendish, the Marquess of Harrington, in 1944 and had become Lady Harrington. Just four months after the couple tied the knot, Cavendish was killed while fighting in World War Two.

In 1948, Kick was travelling from Paris to the French Riviera on holiday with her companion, Lord Harrington, with whom she was romantically involved.

‌



The aircraft flew directly into a storm and its wings were torn off when the pilot abruptly attempted to pull up. All four passengers on board perished.

Chappaquiddick death

View 10 Images Ted lost control of the car and it plunged into water (Image: Popperfoto/Getty Images)

The Chappaquiddick tragedy haunted Ted Kennedy throughout his entire political career. In 1969, five years after his near-fatal plane crash, Ted was driving his campaign worker Mary Jo Kopechne home following a party.

‌



He lost control of the vehicle and plunged into water. Kennedy managed to swim to shore but Mary Jo remained trapped inside.

Rather than alerting police immediately, Ted walked back to his hotel and didn't contact officers until 10 hours after the fatal crash. He pleaded guilty to leaving the scene of an accident and received a two-month suspended sentence.

Ted attempted to justify his actions by saying: "All kinds of scrambled thoughts went through my mind, including such questions as whether some awful curse did actually hang over the Kennedys."

‌



David A Kennedy

View 10 Images David A Kennedy died from an overdose (Image: Getty)

David started using drugs after witnessing the shooting of his father, Robert. His addiction gradually spiralled out of control. He died aged 28 of an overdose in 1984.

He was discovered in a Florida hotel room after overdosing on cocaine, painkillers and anti-psychotic medicine.

‌



Michael Kennedy

Robert's son became embroiled in controversy when, in 1997, he was investigated for statutory rape following an alleged relationship with a 14 year old.

He passed away later that same year, aged 39, in a skiing accident in Colorado where he collided with a tree.

Cousin accused of murder

Bobby Kennedy's nephew, Michael Skakel, was found guilty of carrying out a murder when he was just 15. In 1975 he bludgeoned his neighbour, Martha Moxley, to death while in a jealous rage.

‌



He eventually faced justice in 2002 but was granted a new trial in 2013 and released on bail.

Stillborn son and miscarriages

View 10 Images Jackie Kennedy endured a series of pregnancies (Image: Getty)

First Lady Jackie endured a series of devastating pregnancies before her husband was assassinated.

‌



While her third and fourth pregnancies gave the couple their children Caroline and John, the others ended in miscarriage and stillbirth. Her first pregnancy ended in a miscarriage in 1955.

She fell pregnant once more the following year, but delivered a stillborn baby girl whom the couple chose to name Arabella. Her fifth pregnancy also ended in heartbreak, when the couple's son Patrick Bouvier Kennedy arrived three weeks premature.

Despite the best efforts of medical staff, little Patrick passed away when he was just thirty-nine hours old. Kennedy retreated to the hospital's boiler room and wept. Jackie was too weak to attend the funeral.

Article continues below

During the service Jack removed his Saint Christopher's medal, given to him as a wedding gift, and placed it upon his son's white coffin.

News.title: Beatrice and Eugenie's 'marriage woes' and husband's 'strict rule about Fergie'
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Beatrice and Eugenie's 'marriage woes' and husband's 'strict rule about Fergie'

Princess Beatrice and her husband Edoardo Mapelli Mozzi have been hit by claims that their marriage is in trouble after her parents' fall from grace. Here, The Mirror gets the lowdown on what's really going on behind closed doors...

View 5 Images Princess Beatrice's marriage is said to have hit turbulent times (Image: Max Mumby/Indigo, Getty Images)

Princess Beatrice has had a torrid few months. First her disgraced parents were kicked out of their Royal Lodge home over their affiliation with paedophile Jeffrey Epstein, then dad Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was arrested, and now cruel rumours have surfaced claiming that her marriage is in trouble.

‌



Since the latest tranche of Epstein files revealed the true extent of friendship between Epstein, Andrew and his ex-wife Sarah Ferguson, all eyes have been on the York family. Sarah Ferguson has gone to ground, while Andrew has set up a mobile home at his Sandringham home, reportedly to cater to round the clock staff.

‌



Yesterday, Sarah was stripped of her "Freedom of the City of York" honour after a unanimous vote.

‌



Meanwhile, their daughters Beatrice and Eugenie have been pulled into the fallout after their names cropped up in the millions of documents dropped by the US Congress in the Epstein files. The files indicate that their relationship with Epstein - clearly initiated by their parents - was a long-standing one. However, mentions in the Epstein files are not indicative of wrongdoing.

Sarah Ferguson stripped of honour in latest fallout from Epstein scandal READ MORE :

Shamed Andrew's dogs might be banned from new swamp home thanks to strict rule READ MORE :

View 5 Images Sarah and Andrew with their daughters in happier times, before the Epstein scandal unfolded (Image: Instagram)

One exchange from 2011, three years after his conviction for sex trafficking a minor, showed a correspondent, believed to be Ferguson, tell him how Beatrice believes he should be free to move on with his life. The email was sent after Ferguson publicly denounced Epstein to journalist Geordie Greig, while secretly staying in touch. "Beatrice and I had a discussion and we agreed it was important to call Geordie back and make sure he understood the severity of NOT making a mistake and getting it wrong about Jeffrey, because he was sent to prison for sexual offending, but that he had done his penance and was out of jail and moving on with his life," the email read. "And Geordie was NOT in anyway to go down the P. route. I duly did the call, and Lee Ashley Johnson was also witness to that call as well as Beatrice."

‌



Another exchange in 2010 sees Epstein ask if there is "any chance" either of the sisters "saying hello" to an unnamed person visiting London. In 2015, an unknown account emailed Epstein mentioning that Beatrice would be joining them on a group trip to Mexico. "Oops...in Mexico on the group trip with DK, founder of Twitter etc. and it turns out princess Beatrice will be joining us too.

"I can see the headlines already. :) Do you still talk to them?" the sender wrote. Epstein replied, "she likes me its ok."

Initially, it seemed that the rest of The Firm was backing the sisters. Both attended Christmas at Sandringham with their royal relatives and there was talk that Andrew even agreed to leave Royal Lodge on the proviso that they would be able to remain in the family.

‌



But since then, it has been claimed that despite King Charles's affection for his nieces, they aren't allowed to attend Royal Ascot - and perhaps even will swerve the annual church service for Easter Sunday, and upcoming royal garden parties.

View 5 Images Edoardo Mapelli Mozzi , Princess Eugenie and Princess Beatrice at Sandringham Church last year

An insider told the Daily Mail: "Beatrice and Eugenie are particularly vulnerable at the moment. As they are dragged further into the Epstein web, the King is shifting his stance on the girls' future. Although they aren't implicated in any of Epstein's wrongdoings, their own association with him threatens to tarnish the reputation of the throne."

‌



It's been claimed that the sisters are responding differently to this turn of events. "Both girls are very highly strung and emotional, like their mother. Beatrice is trying to come to terms with it matter-of-factly although can't help bursting into tears regularly - not for her father but for the implosion of her own world and, especially, the family's cold shoulder. Her royal status means more to her than Eugenie," a source told the Mail. On the other hand, Eugenie "has apparently gone into denial - fingers in the ears, head in the sand, hoping it will all go away."

Naturally, the stress of the scandal is said to have impacted their respective marriages. Eugenie's husband, Jack Brooksbank, has reportedly refused to allow her now homeless mum Sarah to move into their Portugal home. A source close to the family said that Jack maintains a "code of honour" which means he won't "completely desert Andrew and Sarah", but equally won't "step into the line of fire himself".

View 5 Images Princess Eugenie and her husband Jack Brooksbank (Image: UK Press via Getty Images)

‌



"His first loyalty has to be to his wife and their two young children. The whole sordid affair has already brought them a great deal of unwarranted attention, and he won't do anything to increase that," the source told The Mail. The boss of a consultancy firm is said to be worried about the potential impact the scandal could have on his business and fears his family will be "tainted by association".

"Jack has worked very hard building his business, and it's going well, so the last thing he needs is for the Epstein scandal to taint that," the source added. "He will do everything he can to keep his head below the parapet because of that."

The couple are reportedly hoping Ferguson won't ask to move in with them. Another source claimed: "Eugenie and Jack are resisting any suggestion from her mother that she should come and live with them.

‌



View 5 Images Princess Beatrice, Edoardo and his son Wolfie

"Because, to be frank, Sarah is difficult to live with and they don't want the responsibility of looking after her at the moment. That's not to say they are not concerned about her welfare - they just don't actually want her to be living with them, even temporarily."

Meanwhile, there are rumours of trouble in paradise for Beatrice and her husband Edoardo Mapelli Mozzi, 42. Beatrice, 37, has been married to Edoardo since 2020 when they tied the knot in socially distanced service during the COVID-19 pandemic. They share two daughters, Sienna, four, and Athena, one, as well as Edo's son Christopher 'Wolfie' - nine years old - from a previous relationship with Dara Huang.

‌



The pair have not been seen together in recent weeks and tongues started wagging after Edoardo left her side in her hour of need to travel 4,500 miles away to Palm Beach, Florida. There, he posted Instagram pictures of himself enjoying a rose against the backdrop of a stylish hotel courtyard.

As the cofounder of a property development and design business - Banda - one 'former associate' claimed to the Mail that his business profile skyrocketed after he started a romance with Beatrice back in 2019: "When they first got together, Edo wasn't nearly as successful. Being adjacent to royalty was transformative."

Back in 2019, accounts from Companies House that indicated the design portion of the business was £244,000 in debt - but now has a turnover of £2.2 million. However, Edo's friends insist he would never name drop his wife or her family.

‌



Now, a source has brutally claimed that there's a 'noticeable distance' between the pair since the Epstein scandal, and that "things haven't been great for a while between them, [but] Beatrice is determined to soldier on and forge a path through this crisis."

The source added that he "seems more and more distracted by work and travel... just when she needs him most, he's been pulling away." However, an insider tells the Mirror that these claims couldn't be further from the mark, with a source close to the couple laughing off the suggestion that their marriage is on the rocks.

"This is nonsense," the source said, while a second told Hello magazine that the businessman's recent trips were nothing to do with the ongoing controversy surrounding Andrew and Fergie - and that instead the couple are doing a very similar juggling act to many parents who have to travel for work.

Article continues below

"The only thing Bea and Edo are navigating are their busy jobs and looking after their children! They've both had recent stints working abroad and like so many working parents, have balancing work with looking after the kids," they said.

"Recent reports that things aren't good between them are not true at all. Things are great between them and the only distance between them recently has been physical distance because of work!"

News.title: Amanda Knox does stand up comedy 'to laugh at things that happened'
News.content:
Amanda Knox does stand up comedy 'to laugh at things that happened'

Amanda Knox was wrongly jailed over the murder of University of Leeds student Meredith Kercher, 21, back in 2007 - she has since incorporated her prison time into comedy sets

View 3 Images Amanda Knox has performed stand-up comedy, joking about her experience (Image: Getty Images for ABA)

Wrongly jailed Amanda Knox is doing stand-up comedy "to laugh at the bad things that have happened."

American Amanda's show features jokes about her time in an Italian jail after she was wrongly jailed over the murder of her British roommate Meredith Kercher. Amanda and her then-boyfriend Raffalle Sollecito and Rudy Guede were also convicted.

Prosecutors at the time alleged student Meredith, 21, died in a "sex game gone wrong" in Perugia, Italy, back in 2007. Amanda returned to the US and was acquitted in 2015.

Amanda, 38, is now a mum-of-two and is sharing her story via comedy sets. In one gag, she joked jail hardships were nothing compared to the challenges of motherhood.

View 3 Images Meredith was killed in Italia in 2007 (Image: ITALIAN POLICE/AFP via Getty Ima)

Amanda Knox boast as she posts comic clip on trial for Meredith Kercher murder READ MORE :

"I think that finding the ability to laugh at the bad things that have happened to you and find the absurdity in the human condition (is very important)," said of her stand-up sets. "I really love how comedy allows me to feel connected to other people.

"It’s a way of turning the temperature down a little bit and making fun of myself." Ivorian Guede ultimately served 16 years for the murder and sexual assault of University of Leeds student Meredith.

View 3 Images Amanda was acquitted years after she was falsely imprisoned (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Amanda's foray into comedy has triggered hundreds of responses, from people on her side as well as those who have blasted her. But it was her reply to one that came across as a sick boast.

Article continues below

Knox previously took to X/Twitter to report a video clip showing a wrestling match in which an Italian semi-pro wrestler threw a pizza in the ring before hurling it at his competitor and appearing to knock him out with it. Reposting the video, Knox added: "This is essentially how my trial went."

One user said in a reply too her post with a video asking if she won, to which Amanda replied: "I got the last laugh." "Nothing funnier than rape and murder, right Amanda?" asked one user. While another said: "Of course you did. Psychopaths like you typically find murder funny."

Amanda returned to the US in 2011 where she went on to complete a degree and wrote a book about the case. Following her exoneration, Knox has become a prominent voice on wrongful convictions, hosting podcasts, writing books, and regularly appearing in the media.

News.title: Man explores Rolf Harris' decaying £4m mansion and makes 'creepy' discovery in basement
News.content:
Man explores Rolf Harris' decaying £4m mansion and makes 'creepy' discovery in basement

A team of urban explorers researching the former home of disgraced children's entertainer Rolf Harris made a bizarre find the in the basement

The Bearded Explorer enters Rolf Harris' derelict mansion

Rolf Harris was a household name in the UK for over 30 years, making Top 10 records, fronting a variety of TV shows and even, in 2005, being commissioned to paint a portrait of Queen Elizabeth II. But the ebullient entertainer had a long-concealed dark side, and in 2014 Harris was convicted on twelve counts of indecent assault on four young female victims.

‌



The presenter of such shows as Rolf's Cartoon Club and Animal Hospital never worked again. Days before what would have been the disgraced entertainer’s 96th birthday on Monday (March 30), an urban explorer has taken a peek at his former home – a now dilapidated mansion that was once valued at over £4million but is now being offered for less than half of that value.

‌



Spanning 6,440 square feet over two floors, the property boasts four bedrooms as well as a 45-foot living space and a 35-foot master bedroom. There is also an artist's studio, a self-contained annexe and a boat house outside. But it is what was in the basement that left YouTuber Colin stunned.

‌



View 9 Images Harris continued to live as a virtual recluse on the property after his conviction (Image: n.c)

On his Bearded Explorer channel, he said: “I can honestly say I wasn't expecting to find what we did down there.” One of the key rules of urban exploration is that explorers should do no harm to the buildings they enter, and should never take anything away with them.

Urbex is generally considered as trespass which is regarded as a civil rather than a criminal matter, and so can lead to court action from landlords. But there are circumstances when those trespassing can be prosecuted for committing criminal offences, such as if they enter through force. Colin told The Mirror he had entered through an unlocked door.

‌



View 9 Images The exterior of Rolf Harris' home (Image: YOUTUBE/BEARDED EXPLORER)

There are also safety issues, with derelict buildings having a higher risk of inadequate flooring, unsafe roofing and more.

Colin says he couldn’t separate the once-luxurious home from its dark history. “It is beautiful here,” he said. “It really is lovely… a lovely place to live, but, sadly… there’s a lot of bad history that's gone on here. Some of the crimes are allegedly actually have supposed to taken place here as well.”

‌



At the rear of a house is a small, now very overgrown boat dock, and Colin managed to find an archive photo of Harris standing proudly in the same location when the house was still an architectural showpiece.

View 9 Images Harris in the garden with his dog Summer (Image: ExpressStar)

The building is “now starting to show signs of decay,” Colin says. “The home is now estimated to need between £1.5 and £2million just in renovations and building work to make it habitable again.”

‌



The custom-designed home was inspired by Harris's childhood home on the banks of the Swan River in Perth, Western Australia. “He'd obviously paid a lot of money to have that done,” Colin said.

View 9 Images Colin and his team were amazed by their find (Image: YouTube/TheBeardedExplorer)

Brian Warren, a local estate agent who previously valued the riverside property after handling sales of neighbouring homes on the same road, previously told the Mirror: "We actually looked at it and others on the same row. I originally said it was worth £2million."

‌



He added that the initial £4m asking price had been pitched too high. He added: "No one buying at that price would want to live in it because of the stigma and it's a mess, with all add-on extensions that Rolf Harris put on over the years. So they're looking at paying another £1.5million on top of that to rebuild it because it's on the river, which is more expensive."

View 9 Images Colin found the mysterious artefact quite disturbing (Image: YouTube/TheBeardedExplorer)

The property has been to a large extent cleared of the disgraced entertainer’s personal effects, but Colin came across one bizarre relic in the building’s basement. “This is Alwen Harris female head,” Colin said, holding up a mould that had clearly been made of a human head. “That's his wife. That's a bit creepy, isn't it?”

‌



He went on: “I mean, it's very strange, isn't it? Like, why would you do that? Why would you want a a mould of your wife's head? And why would you leave it here? And why would you leave it in the basement?”

View 9 Images The sprawling open plan living room (Image: YOUTUBE/BEARDED EXPLORER)

Alwen Hughes was a Welsh sculptress and jeweller who married Harris in 1958 and stood by him after his sex assault conviction. died of a stroke in September 2024, just a year after her husband.

‌



The mould may well have been connected with her work, perhaps part of her preparations for a self-portrait, but Colin found it rather disturbing: “Look at the eyes… that is creepy as you like, isn't it?

View 9 Images One of the many bathrooms (Image: YOUTUBE/BEARDED EXPLORER)

Holding up the strange-looking mould of the late artist’s head, Colin said: “We found some weird things, haven't we? I didn't expect to see that in here!”

‌



After months of attempts, Harris' former home once again went up for auction on Thursday. The price was slashed to £1.95m having initially been put up for sale at £4m last year.

However, once again, it failed to sell. Bids petered out at £1,520,000 and it failed to reach its reserve.

View 9 Images Harris with wife Alwen and then-seven year-old daughter Bindi at their home in 1971 (Image: Daily Mirror)

‌



After his release from prison in 2017, Harris returned to his multi-million pound mansion on the River Thames. Unsurprisingly, neighbours were horrified and raced to put their homes on the market, some with millions off the asking price.

Some of his offences were committed at the property, including sexual assaults on one of daughter Bindi's friends. The victim was aged between 13 and 19 years when she was assaulted between 1968 and 1986. He was said to have performed a sex act on her while his daughter slept in another room.

View 9 Images One of the rooms in Harris' house (Image: YOUTUBE/BEARDED EXPLORER)

Article continues below

She was abused by the entertainer for years and eventually came forward while aged in her 40s. Bindi stood firmly by her dad's side throughout the trial and argued that the 'affair' with her friend didn't start until the woman was 18.

Harris was famously spotted leaving his home by boat along the River Thames en route to his sentencing hearing in a bid to dodge journalists.

It was also where he became a recluse before his the death from neck cancer in 2023.

News.title: Inside abandoned Pontins Southport with rooms left 'frozen in time'
News.content:
Inside abandoned Pontins Southport with rooms left 'frozen in time'

Urban explorer reveals haunting images inside Pontins Southport after the holiday park shut unexpectedly in 2023, leaving apartments and furnishings completely untouched

View 3 Images The park has been left abandoned (Image: Exploring with Matt)

Pontins Holiday Park in Southport abruptly closed its doors for good in 2023, leaving holidaymakers and locals stunned. The site, once teeming with activity as a favoured holiday spot, now stands eerily quiet, its buildings and interiors poignantly echoing happier days.

‌



The holiday flats, still furnished and equipped, cast a spectral glow over the deserted area. Local urban explorer Matt from Rochdale happened upon the desolate scene. Sharing his escapades on social media under 'Exploring with Matt', he's carved out a reputation for himself investigating derelict structures and forgotten lands.

‌



While he usually gravitates towards the hallways of long-abandoned hospitals, Matt couldn't resist the charm of the vacant Pontins park. Recalling his visit, he said: "I'd seen that it had closed online and I just happened to be in the area, so I checked it out along with my best mate No Limits Urbex, a top bloke."

‌



Couple selling dream £4million Omaze mansion just months after getting married in it READ MORE :

View 3 Images The rooms have remained intact (Image: Exploring with Matt)

He stated: "There are big misconceptions about what I do. I am not trying to encourage people to put themselves in danger or take any risks. I am doing that on behalf of people, to give people an insight into the amazing history of some of these buildings that they see every day and don't think about," according to Lancs Live.

"Trespass is a civil matter, so it is between the explorer and the landowner. Often, these buildings have been left for so long it is almost impossible to find out who that land owner is or where they are.

‌



"I find places to go by looking at online websites dedicated to abandoned buildings and there is a huge online urban explorers community. For me, I am just so interested in buildings with a lot of history, such as old theatres, hospitals or abandoned colleges or high schools. They are like time capsules. It is so fascinating to me."

Matt said the Pontins in Southport looked as though "time had stood still", observing that the holiday apartments remain untouched, complete with kitchen gadgets and furnishings inside each flat. Beyond the flats, the images show a modest outdoor play area, featuring wooden swings and a picnic table set against a backdrop of communal greenery.

View 3 Images Matt explored the area (Image: Exploring with Matt)

‌



Bill Esterson is the MP for Sefton Central, the constituency which includes Ainsdale, the location of the closed Pontins site. He said: "Many local people want to see Ainsdale's beach gateway revived. Leisure trends may have shifted, but Ainsdale's village centre is thriving, and the beach remains one of the region's real beauty spots.

"There's an appetite for change, but Britannia must release the lease and allow someone to develop the Pontins site. I'm working with the Council, residents, and MPs facing similar issues with Pontins around the country to find a way forward." Folks have been reflecting on their treasured memories at the Holiday Park, with one individual recalling: "As a child we went here for our family holiday. It was the best, I have so many memories of Pontins, the bluecoats, donkey derby, the boating lake, miss Pontins competition, miss lovely legs competition, the goodnight children song, captain Croc, the party dances. Such a shame its closed."

Another user filled with nostalgia remarked: "The Morris dancing world will heavily miss it. A lot of us spent our championships here every year so it holds some great memories, it will forever be missed."

‌



A third chimed in with their own recollections: "It's a shame it's closed and looks really tidy and clean! This place has probably made so many memories over the years with children making friends and people meeting their life long partners!".

Expressing regret at the closure, another shared: "I was a contractor for a number of years and enjoyed working for Britannia and Pontins and seeing so many holiday makers enjoy their break away! Sadly last year it was quiet and this is mainly down to the cost of living crisis!".

Reflecting on times past, someone mourned: "Such a shame it's come to this, Southport was the flag ship for family holidays in the Pontins group."
