Roy Keane reacts exactly as expected after Rory McIlroy delivers thoughtful gift READ MORE :

However, Trump has insisted Woods will not be in action at the Masters. The White House chief did not reveal his sources, but Woods is in a relationship with Vanessa Trump, the president's former daughter-in-law.

Trump told Fox News: "I love Tiger, but he won't be there. He'll be there, but he won't be playing in it.”

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Woods is yet to confirm whether he will be competing at the Masters, though his profile on the tournament's official website states he will be competing. He has made it clear he will be at Augusta in some capacity and will attend the Champion's Dinner.

The golf great has undergone seven back surgeries and also ruptured his left Achilles tendon in March 2025. After his side's SoFi Cup loss, Woods did open up about how hard he is working to be fit to compete.

View 3 Images Woods was in action at the SoFi Cup, albeit his side were beaten convincingly (Image: 2026 Jamie Squire/TGL)

‌



He said: "I've been trying. It's just this body doesn't recover like when I was 24, 25. It doesn't mean I'm not trying - I've been trying for a while.

"I've had a couple of back injuries the past year that I've had to fight through and it's taken some time. But I keep trying.

View 3 Images Trump and Woods know each other well (Image: 2025 Getty Images)

Article continues below

"I want to play, I've loved the tournament, I've loved being there since I was 19 years old. So it's meant a lot to me and my family over the years and I'm going to be there either way.”

When asked if it will be a late decision, perhaps one in the days counting up to the start of the major, Woods replied: “I don't know, we'll see how it goes. I'll be practicing playing at home this week and keep trying to make progress.”

Sports.title: Rory McIlroy refused Bryson DeChambeau request at Masters as he speaks out on disagreement
Sports.content:
Rory McIlroy refused Bryson DeChambeau request at Masters as he speaks out on disagreement

Tensions between Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau reached new heights at the Masters during a decisive moment

View 2 Images Bryson DeChambeau and Rory McIlroy had a disagreement at the 2025 Masters (Image: Getty Images)

Rory McIlroy has revealed how he refused to give in to pressure from Bryson DeChambeau in the lead up to his Masters victory last year. In the final round of the 2025 Masters, McIlroy’s desperate attempt to finally win at Augusta National saw him implement a new tactic.

‌



The Northern Irishman, both in an effort to maintain focus and to frustrate his opponent, refused to make eye contact with DeChambeau and treated his rival as if he wasn’t there, even wearing AirPods until the moment he stepped onto the first tee. The American had tried to bait him into conversation at various points in order to break his rhythm, leading to a tense atmosphere by the ninth hole.

‌



Both players hit brilliant approaches at the steep uphill par-4, leaving themselves around 12 to 15 feet away for birdie. But there was a disagreement over who was furthest away and who would therefore putt first.

‌



Instead of the usual quick word or a hand gesture to decide the order, McIlroy continued the silent treatment until DeChambeau suggested leaving it to chance by throwing a tee to determine who would go first. McIlroy refused, leaving the LIV Golf star to measure the respective distances with his steps before finally giving in.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

The eventual champ drained the putt to a deafening roar from the crowd, while a visibly agitated DeChambeau stepped up and pushed his putt wide right. That shifted the momentum in McIlroy’s favour as he mounted a four-shot lead.

‌



Golf news: Tiger Woods makes Masters admission as Scottie Scheffler pulls out of event READ MORE :

Is Tiger Woods playing at The Masters after huge hint as he makes golf comeback READ MORE :

Recalling the moment of friction in Amazon Prime’s new documentary, Rory McIlroy: The Masters Wait, he said: "I thought it was very clearly my putt.

"I thought his ball was slightly closer than mine. We sort of looked at each other and I’m like, ‘I think it’s me to go,’ and he’s like, ‘Well, I think it’s me to go.’

"It’s a very gamesmanship-y thing. Really, both of us wanted to putt first. Because if you can hole that putt before your opponent it puts pressure on them.

‌



View 2 Images McIlroy came out on top in a decisive moment at Augusta (Image: Getty)

"He goes, ‘So why don’t we just throw a tee up for it to see who goes up first?’ And I’m like, ‘No, this is the final round of the Masters, this isn’t some game on a Tuesday afternoon somewhere.’

"I wasn’t going to wilt in that situation, I was just going to stand firm. So I said, ‘Why don’t we get someone to come and measure the distance,' and he said, ‘No, no it’s fine. You can go anyway. I don’t care.’

‌



"I was proud of myself for holding my ground."

That moment marked a turning point for DeChambeau as he looked increasingly out of sorts. Later, airing his frustration, he said: "Rory wouldn't talk to me. Not a word. I get being focused but that’s just not me. It felt cold out there."

McIlroy went on to claim his first ever Green Jacket and complete the career Grand Slam following a play-off win over Justin Rose. The five-time major champion was unapologetic in the aftermath, saying: "I wasn’t there to be his mate. I was there to win the Masters. If he wanted a conversation, he should’ve booked a lunch."

Article continues below

Sports.title: Lewis Hamilton learns fate after being summoned to stewards over Max Verstappen incident
Sports.content:
Lewis Hamilton learns fate after being summoned to stewards over Max Verstappen incident

Lewis Hamilton faces no further action from the stewards after an incident with Max Verstappen during the first practice session at Suzuka, despite the Dutchman's furious radio message

View 2 Images Lewis Hamilton did not recieve a warning from Ferrari about Max Verstappen approaching (Image: Clive Rose - Formula 1, Formula 1 via Getty Images)

Lewis Hamilton has escaped punishment from the stewards following an incident with Max Verstappen during the Japanese Grand Prix opening practice session. The flashpoint unfolded between turns 14 and 15 at Suzuka, with Hamilton accused of driving unnecessarily slowly, erratically, or potentially dangerously to other drivers.

‌



Hamilton, 41, was in the centre of the track on a slow lap when Verstappen caught up during a flying lap in his Red Bull. As a result, the Dutchman had to dart down the left side to avoid Hamilton's Ferrari.

‌



Verstappen was far from pleased. He exclaimed over team radio: "What the f**k!"

‌



The FIA's full statement read: "Having reviewed video and team radio, the Stewards find that the Driver of Car 44 (Lewis Hamilton), who had been given no warning from the team of the approach of Car 3 (Max Verstappen), was travelling in the middle of the track between Turn 14 and Turn 15.

"As Car 3 closed on Car 44, Car 44 made an initial movement suggesting a move to the left, but ultimately remained to the right-hand side of the track. Car 3 approached with a significant speed differential but was able to pass Car 44 without being required to brake, lift, or deviate from its intended racing line.

George Russell not happy as Mercedes questioned by FIA after Kimi Antonelli footage READ MORE :

Toto Wolff responds to Max Verstappen rumours after George Russell axe threat READ MORE :

"In this case, the Stewards are satisfied that, although Car 3 was momentarily influenced by the positioning of Car 44, it was not forced to take evasive action. The Stewards also took into account that, through his team representative, the Driver of Car 3 said that the situation was not considered dangerous from his perspective.

‌



"Consistent with the position taken with respect to incidents of this type in free practice sessions where an alleged impeding is not considered dangerous or potentially so, the Stewards determined to take no further action. However, the team is reminded to ensure that timely warnings are given to its drivers of the approach of other cars."

Verstappen also went before the stewards following claims that Franco Colapinto had held up the Red Bull during the second practice session at turn 15.

View 2 Images Max Verstappen dropped an f-bomb over the Red Bull team radio after having to avoid Lewis Hamilton's Ferrari (Image: NurPhoto, NurPhoto via Getty Images)

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

The Alpine driver allegedly violated Article B4.1.1, which states: "Any driver taking part in any LTCs (listed team components) who, in the opinion of the stewards, stops unnecessarily on the circuit or unnecessarily impedes another driver shall be subject to the penalties referred to in Article B1.9.4."

That article states: "In the event of an incident during any LTCS, the Stewards may delete a driver's lap time (or lap times) or drop the driver such number of grid positions as they consider appropriate."

Article continues below

It further states: "Unless it is completely clear that a driver committed a driving infringement, any such incident will normally be investigated after the relevant session." The article additionally notes: "Any such grid position penalties will be served in the race."

Colapinto finished 17th in the session, three positions behind Alpine team-mate Pierre Gasly. Verstappen, by contrast, secured a top-10 spot but was 1.376 seconds adrift of Oscar Piastri, who led the timesheets.

Sports.title: Jurgen Klopp names Liverpool star who could break Mo Salah record in 10 years
Sports.content:
Jurgen Klopp names Liverpool star who could break Mo Salah record in 10 years

Jurgen Klopp was the man who bought Mohamed Salah to Liverpool with the Egyptian going on to score over 250 times - but the German has cited one man who could pass that figure

View 2 Images Mo Salah has confirmed he'll leave Liverpool this summer (Image: Ryan Crockett/DeFodi Images/DeFodi via Getty Images)

Jurgen Klopp has suggested that Hugo Ekitike could surpass Mohamed Salah's Liverpool goals record if he sticks around for the next decade.

‌



The Egyptian announced this week that he would leave Anfield in the summer with the two parties choosing to rip up his contract, which had a year to run, by mutual consent. Despite being in the midst of his worst ever season for Liverpool, plaudits have poured in given what he's achieved.

‌



Salah set a then record for goals in a Premier League season in his debut year and has continued to be prolific, landing the Golden Boot four times well as being named the PFA Player of the Year on three occasions.

‌



Salah is the third highest scorer in Liverpool's history, surpassing the likes of Robbie Fowler and Michael Owen. His total of 255 means he's only behind Roger Hunt (285) and Ian Rush (346) - but Klopp has suggested that this year's top scorer Ekitike could have a shot at eventually eclipsing him.

The Frenchman has made an immediate impact since arriving from Frankfurt and has scored 17 times thus far. He would need to score 25 times a year for the next decade if he was to surpass Salah, which Klopp confessed would be a tall order.

Former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp breaks silence on Mo Salah exit READ MORE :

Jurgen Klopp speaks on Liverpool return as Anfield icon set for emotional reunion READ MORE :

The German told The Anfield Wrap : "I hope he [Salah] can have a successful rest of the season. We texted actually last night, I really hope he enjoys the rest of the season but I know Mo can only enjoy it if you win football matches and he scores. Where he came from, what he went through and what he gave us… incredible.

‌



"In the moment, when you work with him, the bigger view it’s ridiculous, just ridiculous, unmatched numbers, we may sit here in 10 years, maybe Ekitike [will break his record] but it will be difficult. One of the all time greats, definitely, will leave the club in the summer, and let’s see where he will show up next."

View 2 Images Hugo Ekitike has enjoyed a productive first year at Liverpool (Image: 2026 Getty Images)

Klopp has also addressed a story which has previously claimed he wanted to sign Julian Brandt from Borussia Dortmund, not Salah, in the summer of 2017. The Reds would ultimately land the Egyptian from Roma for £34million.

Article continues below

He said: "This is the situation, with that when you go for a winger you talk to seven or eight, and yes, we spoke to Julian Brandt but we spoke to Mo Salah, it’s not he [Brandt] didn’t want it so we took him [Salah], whatever people think I don’t care, but in the moment we were 100 per cent convinced he was the one."

Sports.title: Liverpool legend John Toshack hits back at son after claiming he has dementia
Sports.content:
Liverpool legend John Toshack hits back at son after claiming he has dementia

John Toshack has come out and rejected suggestions he has dementia after his son had claimed the ex-Liverpool and Wales player been diagnosed with the disease

View 3 Images John Toshack has batted away suggestions he has dementia (Image: FADEL SENNA/AFP via Getty Images)

Liverpool and Wales football icon John Toshack has laughed off his son’s claims he has been diagnosed with dementia. The 77-year-old’s wife Mai Angulo admitted yesterday Thailand-based Cameron Toshack’s revelations about his dad’s “dementia battle” had left her astonished.

‌



Confessing Covid had left him with severe physical and mental after-effects, she told Spanish daily El Diario Vasco in an interview: “Cameron only knows what he talks about very occasionally on the phone with his father. He hasn’t seen him for two years. That’s why it really surprised me that he said this.”

‌



Today it was the turn of the footballer-turned globetrotting manager John to rebut the dementia claims of the 56-year-old current assistant coach at Thai League 1 club Buriram United.

‌



Welcoming a reporter from respected Spanish daily El Mundo into the home in Besalu in the Catalan province of Girona which he shares with Mai and their five dogs, the former Real Madrid and Real Sociedad manager insisted: “Dementia? Not yet!” He joked: “I’ve forgotten all the goals I missed but I remember perfectly the ones I scored.”

Liverpool legend John Toshack diagnosed with dementia as son shares heartbreaking update READ MORE :

El Mundo also published footage of the football legend, who played more than 200 matches with Cardiff City before winning nine trophies during an eight-year career with the Reds, reciting the famous team selection quote he made history with following a calamitous Real Madrid away match against Rayo Vallecano when he was managing the Spanish giants.

Laughing and looking relaxed in a Real Sociedad tracksuit as he sat in the sun, John said: “On Mondays I always think I’ll make ten changes to the team. On Tuesdays, seven or eight. By Thursday, it’s four. Friday two, and by Saturday I’m back to starting the same eleven bastards.”

‌



El Mundo journalist Luis Nunez-Villaveiran, who published the footage on his Instagram, said: “John Toshack’s son said in an interview his father suffered dementia. I’ve been with him and he maintains the spark he became known for in clubs like Real Madrid and Real Sociedad.”

View 3 Images John Toshack's wife had responded to recent accusations about her husband (Image: Facebook/John Benjamin Toshack)

He added: “The former manager takes advantage of a risotto and cheesecake lunch to tease his wife about things he doesn’t remember between glasses of Coca-Cola Zero Sugar, and then wink at the guests before flashing another smile.”

‌



Cameron, Toshack’s son from an earlier marriage to Susan Bann, claimed in an interview with the Daily Mail earlier this week his dad was battling dementia.

He said: “It’s a terrible disease. It’s the short-term memory where we’re seeing it – I speak to him most days and if we chat in the afternoon, he might not remember that we also spoke in the morning. But if I ask him about the Liverpool days, or Sociedad or Madrid, the detail is amazing.”

Mai, Toshack’s wife of 33 years, reacted by saying: “John is very calm here. We celebrated his 77th birthday on Sunday, and he was very happy.”

‌



She did admit however, that his battle with Covid had lasting effects: “It is true that Covid left him with very severe after-effects, both physical and mental. Everyone has been able to notice this.

View 3 Images Toshack won La Liga during his time as Real Madrid manager

“We cannot forget that we went through some terrible moments, with John sedated in an intensive care unit, not knowing how he would progress. He always says that he was out of this world for more than ten days and that when, thank God, he came back, he didn’t understand anything.

‌



“He didn’t know what was happening to him or where he was. It wasn’t easy. I spent many days anxious because, except for John’s closest friends, no one showed interest, not even his family."

In a poignant message to her husband’s army of loyal fans, Mai said: “In his name, I want to send a hug to everyone. The only thing I can say is that I am devoted to John so that he can have the best possible life. He is the love of my life, and I will never leave him. Together we came through those terrible days, and together we will always remain in our home.”

Sports.title: Thomas Tuchel has made thoughts clear on recalling Mason Greenwood for England
Sports.content:
Thomas Tuchel has made thoughts clear on recalling Mason Greenwood for England

Thomas Tuchel did not name Mason Greenwood in his latest England squad list

View 2 Images Thomas Tuchel said he was "not thinking" about Mason Greenwood previously (Image: Getty Images)

Thomas Tuchel already made it clear last year that he was not considering calling up Mason Greenwood to the England national team. Tuchel last week announced his 35-man squad for the upcoming international friendlies against Uruguay and Japan, which will be played this Friday and next Tuesday, respectively.

These two games will help form Tuchel’s final judgement on which 26 players he will take to this summer’s World Cup 2026. Marseille forward Greenwood, formerly of Manchester United, has not been called up to the squad.

The 24-year-old has been one of Ligue 1’s most clinical finishers since signing for Marseille in 2024. He won the league’s Golden Boot last season alongside Ousmane Dembele, having scored 21 goals apiece. This season, he has scored 15 goals in the league and 25 across all competitions.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Before his £23million move to France, Greenwood was charged with rape, assault and controlling and coercive behaviour in January 2022 before the charges were later dropped by the Crown Prosecution Service in February 2023. Greenwood denied all allegations.

During his time at United, he was widely seen as a player who would star for club and country for years to come and even made his England debut in 2020 - his only cap for the Three Lions.

Article continues below

Rio Ferdinand urges Man Utd to make FOUR key signings in summer transfer window READ MORE :

But his legal issues seemingly ended his international career with England before it gained any momentum and he has yet to be recalled to the squad. Former national team boss Gareth Southgate outright said ahead of Euro 2024 he wouldn't consider Greenwood.

Current manager Tuchel does not appear to be considering the forward any time soon either, having previously admitted the forward was "not in his thoughts".

View 2 Images Mason Greenwood has only played for England once (Image: Getty Images)

The German said last year: "I have not spoken to him until now. I have not spoken to him or his camp. My understanding was that he tries to play for Jamaica so we didn't give it another thought. He was not in the mix at the moment and he is not in our thoughts for our team."

Greenwood later filed papers in order to switch his international eligibility to Jamaica and has since obtained a Jamaican passport, paving the way for a potential future with the Reggae Boyz. However, he's yet to make his debut for the country, having reportedly not completed the required change of association form.

That’s despite the fact he was expected to be available for selection for Jamaica's World Cup 2026 qualifiers against Bermuda and Trinidad and Tobago last September. Jamaica are still hoping to reach the World Cup as they play New Caledonia for a chance to face Congo DR in a play-off final, the outcome of which will determine their fate.

Article continues below

Sports.title: Inside England World Cup training HQ as bosses DOUBLE size of privacy fences
Sports.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Inside England World Cup training HQ as bosses DOUBLE size of privacy fences

England boss Thomas Tuchel has revealed that Three Lions stars had specifically asked for a 'small and intimate' hotel and for their World Cup operation to be based from one location

View 4 Images England will be based in Kansas City for their World Cup preparations this summer

England's World Cup training camp in the United States is being specially developed with “privacy fences” being DOUBLED in height. The Three Lions are basing themselves just outside Kansas City in their bid for glory this summer. Bosses at the training complex pride themselves on their hidden, tucked away, location but already have plans in place to make it even more secret. After all, the Swope Soccer Village will be the place where England players will practise taking penalties every day ahead of potential shoot-outs.

‌



Manager Thomas Tuchel is leaving nothing to chance as he bids to bring the FIFA World Cup back to England for the first time since 1966. He said they will treat the complex as home this summer saying: “we try to be as often in Kansas as possible.”

‌



Thomas Tuchel warns England hopefuls they are in last-chance saloon for World Cup spot READ MORE :

Thomas Tuchel makes Ben White plea to England fans as Arsenal star makes Three Lions return READ MORE :

The Swope Soccer Village is already starting to prepare for England’s arrival in early June. FA chiefs have been liaising with the local community to ensure they get the HQ to make the players happy. They have already chosen the small Inn at Meadowbrook - a £200-a-night boutique hotel - to give the players as much as privacy as possible They will be bussed to the nearby Swope Soccer Village were they will train. Local boss Jared Belzer said: “One of the best perks about Swope is the privacy.”

‌



But he said the fences are likely to double in height - it’s one of several changes they’re making to prepare for the Three Lions.

View 4 Images Swope Soccer Village where England will train behind huge fences for World Cup (Image: NCAA Photos via Getty Images)

He added: “It’s got all the amenities you could ask for. However, to accommodate for something like the English National Team there’s got to be some changes.

‌



“So, we really just want England to have a home away from home here at Swope Soccer Village. When they’re here, they’ll have complete access to our fields.”

Crews are to build a studio for the ‘Lions’ Den’ on the grass field and add an outdoor gym. Alan Dietrich said the weight room inside will transform into a Players’ Lounge. He said: “In the summer they brought their coach, coaching staff and CEO back to Kansas City.

“It was at that point and time that the whole crew really bought into Kansas City as the home for the World Cup.” Dietrich said the Three Lions were drawn to the people in Kansas City and player flow on campus at Swope.

‌



He said Swope was not part of the original bid, but when England expressed interest, it was a “no brainer.”

View 4 Images The Inn at Meadowbrook where the England team will stay (Image: The Inn at Meadowbrook)

He explained: “We talked about it and were like: ‘Alright, City, are you prepared to handle this?’ ‘Yes.’

‌



‘Ownership, are you prepared to handle this?’ ‘Absolutely,’ Dietrich said. So then, we thought, we’ll find another place for Sporting to train during this window and we’ll have England base camp here.”

Since opening in 2007, Swope has served as a training site for the U.S. Men’s and Women’s National Teams, KC Current, Sporting KC and the Kansas City Wizards.

Tuchel spoke about his delight over the selection of the hotel and training facilities. The hotel has no golf course but there are several top quality ones nearby. There’s an outdoor pool and a hot tub.

‌



A well-stocked wine cellar includes a £750 Californian Red while, if England go all the way, they will find a £190 Rose champagne to toast their success.

View 4 Images England players wanted a small and intimate hotel for their World Cup base (Image: The Inn at Meadowbrook)

He said: “We're trying to because, basically, that was the choice: to have a home, to have a home base, to have a bed that you're used to sleeping in, to have a bed with a good mattress, to have a hotel with privacy, a small hotel.

‌



“Not a 400, 500, 800 bed hotel where we see each other maybe just in the elevators or on the floor between breakfast and meeting, air-conditioning is on and you cannot open the windows.

“There are a lot of these hotels in America and I think that makes a difference, so we chose a hotel where you can open the window, we chose a hotel where it's an intimate and small place, I think once we get used to that place, it makes sense to go back.

“I have feedback from the players that they like that we start late, that they like that it then becomes condensed, so you have no chance to get bored once you go hopefully through the tournament, looking at it for longer than just the players.

Article continues below

"I had the feeling that a day more could be quite useful so, yeah, these are some decisions that had to be made and maybe the headline is: ‘we try to be as often in Kansas as possible.’”
